rankings.title: Is Industrial Processing the Real Problem With Seed Oils? | Layne Norton, Ph.D.
summary_bullets: Hexane used to extract seed oils.
Residual hexane levels under 1 ppm.
Toxicity requires consuming 11,340 kg oil.
Processing removes some impurities.
Linoleic acid intake rose from 3% to 10%.
rankings.display_title: Is Industrial Processing the Real Problem With Seed Oils? | Layne Norton, Ph.D.

rankings.title: 397 - Endometriosis and adenomyosis: diagnosis, fertility, reproductive aging, & emerging treatments
rankings.speakers: Endometriosis and adenomyosis: diagnosis, fertility, reproductive aging, & emerging treatments
summary_bullets: Endometriosis affects 10% of reproductive women.
Diagnosis delay averages 5–12 years.
Adenomyosis is endometrial tissue in uterine muscle.
Progesterone resistance complicates treatment.
Early diagnosis improves outcomes and preserves fertility.

rankings.title: 395 – Brain lipidology: understanding APOE, cholesterol homeostasis, Alzheimer’s disease, & more
summary_bullets: Brain cholesterol is independent from blood cholesterol.
APOE4 impairs cholesterol transport in brain.
Statins may reduce Alzheimer’s risk.
Omega-3s enter brain via lysophospholipids.
CETP inhibitors show promise for brain health.

rankings.title: A guide to cardiorespiratory training at any fitness level to improve longevity (AMA 79 sneak peek)
summary_bullets: Cardiorespiratory fitness is strongest predictor of longevity.
Zone 2 training builds base for sustained endurance.
V2 max declines ~10% per decade with age.
High intensity per minute gives more adaptation, but zone 2 allows more volume.
Lactate thresholds define training zones.
rankings.display_title: A guide to cardiorespiratory training at any fitness level to improve longevity (AMA 79 sneak peek)

rankings.title: 378 ‒ Women’s health & performance: how training, nutrition, & hormones interact across life stages
summary_bullets: Bone density peaks by age 19 in women.
Menstrual cycle phases affect training and recovery.
Perimenopause is a critical window for muscle quality.
GLP-1 drugs require deliberate nutrition and resistance training.
High-intensity intervals are efficient for time-constrained women.
rankings.display_title: 378 ‒ Women’s health & performance: how training, nutrition, & hormones interact across life stages

rankings.title: 380 ‒ The seed oil debate: are they uniquely harmful relative to other dietary fats?
summary_bullets: Examines seed oil health evidence
Reviews RCTs and mechanistic data
Concludes seed oils not uniquely harmful
Focus on overall diet quality
rankings.display_title: 380 ‒ The seed oil debate: are they uniquely harmful relative to other dietary fats?

rankings.title: How Busy Moms Can Build Strength and Cardio Fitness | Abbie Smith-Ryan, Ph.D.
summary_bullets: Prioritize resistance training 2 days/week, 30 min each.
High-intensity intervals: 1 min on, 1 min off, up to 10 rounds.
Use perceived effort or power, not heart rate, for intervals.
Low-intensity movement fills remaining time.
Resistance: 6–8 reps at 60–80% 1RM, 30 sec rest.
rankings.display_title: How Busy Moms Can Build Strength and Cardio Fitness | Abbie Smith-Ryan, Ph.D.

rankings.title: How Early Training Choices Shape Women’s Health for Life | Abbie Smith-Ryan, Ph.D.
summary_bullets: Osteoporosis starts in childhood bone development.
Early exercise builds lifelong fitness base.
Menstruation often causes girls to leave sports.
Resistance training prevents injury in young athletes.
Asymmetric sports can cause spinal curvature.
rankings.display_title: How Early Training Choices Shape Women’s Health for Life | Abbie Smith-Ryan, Ph.D.

rankings.title: How to Actually Start a Habit | James Clear
summary_bullets: Make habits tiny (one push-up, five minutes).
Redesign environment to reduce friction.
Praise good behaviors, ignore bad ones.
Use momentum from one habit to start another.
Environment changes need only brief motivation.
rankings.display_title: How to Actually Start a Habit | James Clear

rankings.title: 396 – Breast cancer screening: understanding risk, deciding when to start, and more
summary_bullets: Breast cancer kills 42,000 US women yearly.
Screening reduces mortality up to 40%.
Risk assessment should start by age 25.
Annual mammography better than biennial for individuals.
MRI underutilized for high-risk women.

rankings.title: Cooking with Lard vs Seed Oils | Layne Norton, Ph.D.
summary_bullets: Seed oils not inherently toxic.
Both lard and seed oils bad for frying.
Focus on overall diet, not just oil.
Oxidation risk higher in thin-layer frying.
Saturated fat reduction more important than oil type.
rankings.display_title: Cooking with Lard vs Seed Oils | Layne Norton, Ph.D.
